pr.title: lb: implement lb_80019628; improve four lbbgflash functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Implemented a C body for lb_80019628 in lb_0192.c and made several fuzzy-match-oriented improvements in lbbgflash.c. The PR reports no 100% matches, but improved lb_80019628 and multiple lbbgflash functions, with a green configure/ninja build claimed by the author and a bot report showing five unmatched-item improvements.

key_files.role: Adds C implementation of lb_80019628 for pad-sampling-rate period maintenance and alarm rescheduling.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: lb_0192.c summary and Functions matched section

key_files.role: Adjusts several lbbgflash functions for fuzzy matching, including per-channel saturation locals, squared-component order, volatile rotation pointer use, and a reported parent conditional rewrite.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: lbbgflash.c summary and Functions matched section

what_changed.text: lb_80019628 now snapshots lb_804329F0.x38, updates lb_804329F0.x0[0].x0, bounds/reset-checks x8, computes a period from OS_TIMER_CLOCK, x0[0].x0, x0[1].x0, and an approximately 1/60-second cap, then updates PADSetSamplingRate and recreates/sets a periodic OSAlarm using fn_800195FC.
fn_8001FC08 changed repeated RGBA saturation blocks to use branch-local u8 target and f32* cur locals before comparing/storing the current channel value.
lbBgFlash_80020E38 reordered squared local declarations and the zero-vector sum from dz2+dy2+dx2 to dx2+dy2+dz2.

fn_8002113C routes &rot through a Quaternion* volatile rot_ptr and uses that pointer for Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation, HSD_MkRotationMtx, HSD_QuatLib_8037EB28, and FakeHSD_JObjSetRotation in the non-quaternion branch.
The PR body reports fn_80020AEC was changed from a ternary parent assignment to explicit if/else to match branch scheduling, but the loaded raw diff excerpt does not show that hunk.

smart_moves.text: The author kept lb_80019628 as understandable typed C around OSTime, OSAlarm, PADSetSamplingRate, OSCancelAlarm, OSCreateAlarm, and OSSetPeriodicAlarm instead of leaving an asm-only fallback.

For fn_8001FC08, repeated per-channel logic was not looped; instead the PR used narrow branch-local locals to influence MWCC register allocation while preserving the repeated channel shape visible in the prior source.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: MWCC keeps byte target and x10[i] pointer in r5/r4

smart_moves.text: The PR documented fuzzy percentages and explicitly stated no 100% matches, avoiding overclaiming.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Branch-local temporaries can be a narrow matching tactic when MWCC needs to keep a byte target and a pointed-to float current value live in specific registers across similar rising/falling branches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: u8 target and f32* cur introduced in each branch

decomp_lessons.lesson: Declaration order and expression order for squared components can affect floating-point scheduling enough to move fuzzy match score, even when the arithmetic is semantically equivalent.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: dx2/dy2/dz2 reorder and sum reorder

decomp_lessons.lesson: A volatile pointer to an otherwise known local address may prevent MWCC from proving a pointer non-null and eliding inline-substituted assert behavior, but this is a high-risk matching tactic that needs narrow evidence.
Ternary and if/else forms can schedule branches differently; the PR body reports an explicit if/else was used to match a bne / b / li sequence for a parent pointer assignment.

naming_conventions.text: Short local role names such as target and cur were used for per-channel saturation code where the roles are directly supported by surrounding field accesses.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8001FC08 target and cur locals

naming_conventions.text: Squared vector component locals used dx2, dy2, and dz2, matching their direct component-square roles.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbBgFlash_80020E38 dx2 dy2 dz2 locals

naming_conventions.text: rot_ptr was used for a pointer to the local Quaternion rot; the name is role-based and narrow.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use branch-local u8 target and f32* cur locals in repeated saturation branches to influence register allocation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.claimed_effect: PR body says MWCC keeps the byte target and x10[i] pointer in r5/r4 across rising/falling logic.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Reorder floating-point local declarations and sum operands to affect lfs/fmuls/fadds scheduling.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.claimed_effect: PR body says this matches the original floating pipeline better; fuzzy score for lbBgFlash_80020E38 improved from 98.46% to 98.52%.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: lbBgFlash_80020E38 summary and Functions matched

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Route &rot through a Quaternion* volatile local to keep inline-substituted jobj.h asserts from being optimized away.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.claimed_effect: PR body says this preserves asserts at jobj.h:699/700 in Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation expansion; fuzzy score for fn_8002113C improved from 93.17% to 94.72%.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: fn_8002113C summary and Functions matched

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit if/else instead of a ternary parent assignment to influence branch order.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.claimed_effect: PR body says this matched a bne / b / li sequence for fn_80020AEC and improved fuzzy score from 88.27% to 88.77%.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: fn_80020AEC summary and Functions matched

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Bot report for GALE01 showed five improvements in unmatched items: lb_80019628 0.00% to 82.58%, fn_8001FC08 89.68% to 91.77%, fn_8002113C 93.17% to 94.72%, lbbgflash .sdata 50.00% to 65.71%, and lbBgFlash_80020E38 98.46% to 98.52%.
No loaded human review comments or review records were present in this PR slice.

follow_up_queries: fn_8001FC08 branch-local target cur r5 r4
lbBgFlash_80020E38 dx2 dy2 dz2 lfs fmuls fadds
fn_80020AEC parent ternary explicit if else bne b li

evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: PR body plus bot report
notes: Strong evidence for changed files, lb_80019628 body, fn_8001FC08 changes, lbBgFlash_80020E38 reorder, fn_8002113C volatile pointer change, and bot-reported fuzzy improvements.

The PR body states fn_80020AEC changed and improved, but the loaded raw_diff excerpt marked not truncated does not include a fn_80020AEC hunk, and the bot report does not list fn_80020AEC; that claim is retained as PR-body evidence only.
Verification is author-reported as python configure.py && ninja green with build/GALE01/main.dol OK; no independent build logs beyond the loaded text were inspected.
No 100% match claims were made; the PR explicitly reports fuzzy improvements only.
No human review feedback was loaded.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Branch-local locals can steer MWCC register allocation in repeated channel saturation code
accepted_candidate_records.text: In fn_8001FC08, the PR changed direct field loads/stores to branch-local u8 target and f32* cur locals for each RGBA channel; the author states this kept the byte target and x10[i] pointer in r5/r4 and improved the fuzzy score.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Floating-point declaration and operand order can affect scheduling
accepted_candidate_records.text: In lbBgFlash_80020E38, reordering dx2/dy2/dz2 locals and the zero-vector sum coincided with a small fuzzy-score improvement and was described as matching the original lfs/fmuls/fadds pipeline.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Volatile pointer workaround preserved inline assert behavior in one HSD_JObj rotation case
accepted_candidate_records.text: In fn_8002113C, a Quaternion* volatile local was used for &rot so MWCC would not prove the pointer non-null and elide Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation inline assert behavior, according to the PR body.

source_update_candidates.title: Document volatile pointer locals as narrow evidence-required matching tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2508 provides a concrete case where a Quaternion* volatile local was used to stop MWCC from proving &rot non-null and eliding inline-substituted jobj.h assert behavior. If promoted, this should be framed as an exceptional tactic requiring objdiff/build evidence, not normal style.
source_update_candidates.reason: The current standards already warn that volatile locals need evidence; this PR may be a useful example for that standard, but should not weaken the general warning.

source_update_candidates.title: Add example that declaration and operand order can influence MWCC floating-point scheduling
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2508 reports that changing dz2/dx2/dy2 to dx2/dy2/dz2 and changing the sum order helped match the original lfs/fmuls/fadds pipeline in lbBgFlash_80020E38.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful as a narrow matching example; not enough evidence to make a broad rule beyond checking local assembly and verifier output.

source_update_candidates.title: Investigate lbbgflash .sdata improvement not explained by loaded diff summary
source_update_candidates.text: The bot report lists lbbgflash .sdata improving from 50.00% to 65.71%, but the PR body focuses on text fuzzy improvements and the loaded diff does not clearly explain the .sdata change.
source_update_candidates.reason: Curator may want to avoid treating this as an intentional data-section lesson without more evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not promote PR #2508 as evidence of any 100% function match; the PR explicitly says no 100% matches.
Do not infer reviewer approval details or human review intent; no human review comments were loaded.
Do not treat volatile pointer locals as normal style from this PR alone; the evidence supports only a narrow matching tactic with claimed build/fuzzy evidence.
Do not promote the fn_80020AEC tactic as strongly as the diff-backed changes unless another artifact confirms the missing hunk.
